title: linaria alpina subsp. alpina
intro: Linaria alpina subsp. alpina, also known as the Alpine Toadflax, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its bright yellow and blue flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Linaria alpina subsp. alpina include soothing sore throats, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
